steps.title: Identify the initial value
steps.content: An exponential equation has the form $y = a \cdot b^x$, where $a$ is the starting value. The problem tells us that when $x=0$, $y=70$. In this form, when $x=0$, we get $y = a \cdot b^0 = a \cdot 1 = a$, so $a = 70$. This eliminates choices C and D, both with coefficient 50.

steps.title: Identify the growth factor
steps.content:
The problem states for every increase in the value of $x$ by 1, the corresponding value of $y$ increases by 50% of its previous value. An increase of 50% means multiplying by $(1 + 0.50) = 1.50$ each step.

So the base is $b = 1.50$, giving us $y = 70(1.50)^x$, which matches choice A — A is correct.
